scenarios.signal: Sri Lanka's external debt service ratio remains elevated relative to foreign exchange reserves.
scenarios.context: Post-2022 default recovery continues; IMF Extended Fund Facility tranches are contingent on structural benchmarks including SOE reform and revenue measures.
scenarios.implication: Delayed tranche disbursement heightens rollover risk in 2025–2026 window. Bilateral creditor negotiations with China and India remain the critical path.
scenarios.action: Monitor monthly CBSL reserve publications and IMF Article IV consultations. Flag any deviation from fiscal primary balance targets as early warning signal.

scenarios.signal: Sri Lanka's Digital Economy Act and Data Protection Act are creating new regulatory frameworks for platform operators.
scenarios.context: Legislative alignment with GDPR-adjacent standards positions Sri Lanka for potential EU adequacy conversations; however, enforcement capacity remains nascent.
scenarios.implication: Firms operating data-intensive services face compliance ambiguity. First-mover advantage for organizations building compliant infrastructure now.
scenarios.action: Engage Data Protection Authority guidance as it emerges. Build data minimization and consent architecture ahead of enforcement readiness.

meta.methodology: Monte Carlo sampling + linear trend extrapolation
meta.disclaimer: Pre-computed at build time. Not financial or investment advice.
